name: open-meteo-mcp-server
description: Geocode places, fetch global weather forecasts, ERA5 historical climate, marine conditions, air quality, and terrain elevation via MCP. STDIO or Streamable HTTP.

interface.shortDescription: Geocode places, fetch global weather forecasts, ERA5 historical climate, marine conditions, air quality, and terrain elevation via MCP. STDIO or Streamable HTTP.
interface.longDescription: Keyless access to the Open-Meteo global weather APIs over MCP. Resolve place names to coordinates with openmeteo_geocode, then pull forecasts up to 16 days out (openmeteo_get_forecast), the ERA5 historical archive back to 1940 (openmeteo_get_historical), marine wave and swell conditions (openmeteo_get_marine), CAMS air quality and AQI (openmeteo_get_air_quality), Copernicus DEM terrain elevation (openmeteo_get_elevation), probabilistic ensemble runs (openmeteo_get_ensemble), GloFAS river-discharge flood data (openmeteo_get_flood), and bias-corrected daily CMIP6 climate projections 1950–2050 (openmeteo_get_climate). Every weather tool takes latitude/longitude, so geocode first for name-based queries. Large historical, ensemble, flood, or climate pulls spill to a DataCanvas table queryable with SQL via openmeteo_dataframe_describe and openmeteo_dataframe_query when CANVAS_PROVIDER_TYPE=duckdb. Runs over STDIO or Streamable HTTP. No API key required for non-commercial use; weather data by Open-Meteo.com under CC BY 4.0.
